pick.rationale: Iowa State enters as a dominant 2-seed with elite 5th-ranked SRS metrics and a top-10 defense nationally, having compiled a 27-7 record against the nation's toughest Big 12 schedule. While the loss of All-Big 12 guard Keshon Gilbert to a groin injury is significant, the Cyclones' depth and overwhelming statistical advantages present a mismatch that Tennessee State cannot overcome. The Tigers captured a feel-good OVC championship for their first NCAA bid in 32 years, but their mid-major profile faces a gap of over 100 ranking spots against Iowa State's power-conference pedigree, aligning with historical 2/15 outcomes where favorites prevail approximately 94% of the time.

reasoningStep.title: Iowa State vs Tennessee State Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Analysis of 2/15 matchup reveals overwhelming statistical advantages for Iowa State despite Keshon Gilbert's tournament-ending injury. Tennessee State's 32-year NCAA drought and OVC championship are inspiring but insufficient to overcome the elite SRS, top-10 defense, and Big 12-tested pedigree gap.
evidence: Iowa State: 27-7 record, SRS 27.15 (5th), Defensive Rating 95.6 (6th), 3rd place Big 12
Key injury: Keshon Gilbert (All-Big 12 third-team) out with groin/muscle strain
Tennessee State: 23-9 OVC champions, first NCAA bid since 1994 (32-year drought)

result.text: Get To Know The 2026 OVC Tournament Champs: Tennessee State Basketball Skip to content CBB Review (College Basketball Review) College Basketball Power Rankings, Interviews and more! ACC Boston College Cal Clemson Duke Florida State Georgia Tech Louisville Miami (Fla) NC State North Carolina Notre Dame Pitt SMU Stanford Syracuse Virginia Virginia Tech Wake Forest Big East Butler Creighton DePaul Georgetown Marquette Providence Seton Hall St. John’s UConn Villanova Xavier Big Ten Illinois Indiana Iowa Maryland Michigan Michigan State Minnesota Nebraska Northwestern Ohio State Oregon Penn State Purdue Rutgers UCLA USC Washington Wisconsin Big 12 Arizona Arizona State Baylor BYU Cincinnati Colorado Houston Iowa State Kansas Kansas State Oklahoma State TCU Texas Tech UCF Utah West Virginia SEC Alabama Arkansas Auburn Florida Georgia Kentucky LSU Mississippi State Missouri Oklahoma Ole Miss South Carolina Tennessee Texas Texas A&M Vanderbilt Mid Majors AAC Atlantic Sun Atlantic 10 C-USA Horizon MAAC MAC Missouri Valley Mountain West SoCon WAC WCC Oregon State Washington State Bracketology Matty Brackets March on CBB Review Rankings Meet Our Staff Get To Know The 2026 OVC Tournament Champs: Tennessee State Basketball By Mat Mlodzinski Mar 9, 2026 Tennessee State basketball logo First-year head coach Nolan Smith guided Tennessee State basketball to its first NCAA Tournament appearance since 1994. It’s been a long time coming for the Tigers – 32 years in the making – since their last trip to the big dance. Now, guided by former Duke point guard Nolan Smith, they’re heading back to the madness after winning the Ohio Valley Conference Championship. How they got there: Tennessee State basketball went 15-5 in the OVC, taking the tiebreaker over Morehead State, and earning the 1 seed. That got them a bye to the semifinals, where they shut down UT Martin to 55 points on 37.5% shooting. The Tigers only had to win one more game, and advanced to the NCAA Tournament in style, with a 93-67 win over Morehead State. Three Tigers were named to the All-Tournament Team: Travis Harper II, Dante Harris, and Aaron Nkrumah. Harris, who began his college basketball journey at Georgetown in 2020, was named OVC Tournament MVP. General information: Team: Tennessee State Location: Nashville, TN Record: 23-9 (15-5) Head Coach: Nolan Smith (1st season) What to know about Tennessee State basketball: Tennessee State basketball has been playing in Division I since the 1977-78 season, but had only previously made the NCAA Tournament in 1993 and 1994. Those Tigers teams were led by future NBA big man Carlos Rogers and would each be eliminated in the first round. Since that last tournament appearance, it’s been a tough road for the Tigers. They’ve won just 20+ games in a season twice in 2012 and 2016, before a program record 23 wins and counting this year. And of course, they’re led by Nolan Smith, who at 37 years old is one of the youngest

ACC Boston College Cal Clemson Duke Florida State Georgia Tech Louisville Miami (Fla) NC State North Carolina Notre Dame Pitt SMU Stanford Syracuse Virginia Virginia Tech Wake Forest Big East Butler Creighton DePaul Georgetown Marquette Providence Seton Hall St. John’s UConn Villanova Xavier Big Ten Illinois Indiana Iowa Maryland Michigan Michigan State Minnesota Nebraska Northwestern Ohio State Oregon Penn State Purdue Rutgers UCLA USC Washington Wisconsin Big 12 Arizona Arizona State Baylor BYU Cincinnati Colorado Houston Iowa State Kansas Kansas State Oklahoma State TCU Texas Tech UCF Utah West Virginia SEC Alabama Arkansas Auburn Florida Georgia Kentucky LSU Mississippi State Missouri Oklahoma Ole Miss South Carolina Tennessee Texas Texas A&M Vanderbilt Mid Majors AAC Atlantic Sun Atlantic 10 C-USA Horizon MAAC MAC Missouri Valley Mountain West SoCon WAC WCC Oregon State Washington State Bracketology Matty Brackets March on CBB Review Rankings Meet Our Staff Get To Know The 2026 OVC Tournament Champs: Tennessee State Basketball By Mat Mlodzinski Mar 9, 2026 Tennessee State basketball logo First-year head coach Nolan Smith guided Tennessee State basketball to its first NCAA Tournament appearance since 1994. It’s been a long time coming for the Tigers – 32 years in the making – since their last trip to the big dance. Now, guided by former Duke point guard Nolan Smith, they’re heading back to the madness after winning the Ohio Valley Conference Championship. How they got there: Tennessee State basketball went 15-5 in the OVC, taking the tiebreaker over Morehead State, and earning the 1 seed. That got them a bye to the semifinals, where they shut down UT Martin to 55 points on 37.5% shooting. The Tigers only had to win one more game, and advanced to the NCAA Tournament in style, with a 93-67 win over Morehead State. Three Tigers were named to the All-Tournament Team: Travis Harper II, Dante Harris, and Aaron Nkrumah. Harris, who began his college basketball journey at Georgetown in 2020, was named OVC Tournament MVP. General information: Team: Tennessee State Location: Nashville, TN Record: 23-9 (15-5) Head Coach: Nolan Smith (1st season) What to know about Tennessee State basketball: Tennessee State basketball has been playing in Division I since the 1977-78 season, but had only previously made the NCAA Tournament in 1993 and 1994. Those Tigers teams were led by future NBA big man Carlos Rogers and would each be eliminated in the first round. Since that last tournament appearance, it’s been a tough road for the Tigers. They’ve won just 20+ games in a season twice in 2012 and 2016, before a program record 23 wins and counting this year.
